en: In order for Skydance to get its merger approved by Brendan Carr's FCC, it committed to adopt measures
ko: 스카이댄스가 브렌든 카의 FCC에서 합병 승인을 받으려면 국민 뉴스 미디어의 신뢰를 훼손한

en: that can root out the bias that has undermined trust in the national news media.
One of those measures was installing this former conservative think tank's CEO as CBS News' new ombudsman.
But this Monday, Alison took a much bigger step.
ko: CEO를 CBS 뉴스의 새 옴부즈맨으로 임명하는 거였어. 하지만 월요일 앨리슨은 훨씬 큰 조치를 취했어.

en: A major shift in the landscape of TV news.
ko: TV 뉴스 지형의 큰 변화. 파라마운트 스카이댄스가 바리 와이스를

en: Paramount Skydance announcing that they've made Barry Weiss editor-in-chief of CBS News
and acquired her online news and commentary organization, The Free Press. She's a registered independent
ko: CBS 뉴스 편집장으로 임명하고 그녀의 온라인 뉴스 매체 프리 프레스를 인수했다고 발표했어.

en: who at one point called herself politically homeless, unrepresented by the two main U.S. parties.
ko: 등록된 무소속으로 한때 자신을 "정치적 노숙자"라고 불렀어. 미국 양당 어디에도 대표되지 않는다고.

en: Okay, first, politically homeless? Sounds like how you'd describe Rudy Giuliani's
ko: 먼저 "정치적 노숙자"는 루디 줄리아니의 현재 패션 감각을 묘사하는 말 같지만.

en: current fashion sense, but... But it is true.
Paramount has bought The Free Press for $150 million, and Barry Weiss, its co-founder, will now be setting
ko: 파라마운트가 프리 프레스를 1억 5천만 달러에 사고 공동 창업자 바리 와이스가

en: the editorial strategy, vision, and focus for CBS Morning, CBS Evening News, Face the Nation,
ko: CBS 모닝, CBS 이브닝 뉴스, 페이스 더 네이션, 60 미닛의 편집 전략과 비전을 결정하게 돼.

en: and even 60 Minutes. Also, instead of reporting to the president of CBS News,
ko: CBS 뉴스 사장이 아니라 데이비드 엘리슨에게 직접 보고한대.

en: Weiss will apparently report directly to David Ellison.
And look, if you are not familiar with Barry Weiss, you should know she's a proud contrarian
ko: 바리 와이스를 모른다면 알아야 해. 공개 석상에서 이런 버전을 꼭 말하는 자랑스러운 반골이야.

en: who in public appearances will inevitably deliver some version of this pitch.
I confound people because I'm Jewish, I've always thought I was liberal,
I'm gay, I'm married to a woman, like, that's my life.
ko: 진보 같아 보이고 같은 생각을 하고 같은 식당에 가지만 모든 걸 같게 생각하진 않아.

en: You would think that I'm liberal and I would just go along with the flow. It's like, I look like them,
ko: 동의하지 않을 때 두려워하지 않고 그게 사람들을 정말 화나게 한 것 같아.

en: I think like them, I eat at the same restaurants, but I don't think all the same things.
ko: 동의하지 않을 때 두려워하지 않고 그게 사람들을 정말 화나게 한 것 같아.

en: And I'm not scared to say when I disagree.
And I think that's really pissed people off throughout my career.
ko: 먼저 그리고 가장 덜 중요한 건 비트윈 투 펀즈 리부트가 끔찍해 보여.

en: Okay, first and least importantly, the reboot of Between Two Ferns looks terrible, but...
ko: 그게 그녀의 기본 자기소개야. 다른 진보와 의견이 다를 만큼 용감한 진보.

en: That is her basic self. She's a liberal who's brave enough to disagree with other liberals. And along the way,
ko: 팬이 제프 베조스부터 메건 매케인, 에이미 코니 바렛, 조 로건까지 있어.

en: she's accumulated admirers from Jeff Bezos to Meghan McCain, and from Amy Coney Barrett to Joe Rogan.
And if you're thinking, wow, that's not a very ideologically broad from and to, you're starting to catch on.
ko: "와 그 from과 to가 이념적으로 별로 넓지 않은데"라고 생각한다면 알아채기 시작한 거야.

en: Because there are times where she's a little more explicit about what she believes, like when she gave a speech
to the conservative Federalist Society, featuring this fun joke.
ko: 게이 여성이고 적당히 낙태 찬성이에요.

en: I'm a gay woman who is moderately pro-choice.
ko: 이 방에 내 결혼이 합법이면 안 됐다고 생각하는 사람이 있다는 거 알아요.

en: I know that there are some people in this room who don't believe that my marriage should have been legal.
ko: 괜찮아요. 우리 다 세금 낮추길 원하는 미국인이니까.

en: And that's okay, because we're all Americans who want lower taxes.
-♪♪♪♪ Good one. And you can almost hear that room unclench
ko: 그 방이 긴장을 풀었다는 게 거의 들려. 기본적으로 내 권리가 적어야 한다고 생각하지만 걱정 마 베스티 우리 아직 쿨이라고.

en: after she basically says, I know you think I deserve fewer rights, but don't worry, besties, we're still chill.
ko: 당연히 농담으로 프레이밍됐지만 진지하게 서구를 위한 싸움에서 진짜 동맹이 누구인지 안다는 세팅이기도 해.

en: And while that is obviously framed as a joke, it's also a setup to her saying in all seriousness,
I am here because I know that in the fight for the West, who my allies really are.
And she goes on to say, among other things, the wave of so-called progressive prosecutors
ko: "정치적 노숙자"라고 했지만 그 방에서 꽤 편안해 보이더라.

en: has proven to be an immensely bad thing for law and order.
And I've got to say, for someone who's claimed they are politically homeless,
she sure seems awfully at home in that room.
